$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:feedback_item block (Feedback Triage workflow). Validated by scripts/apodictic_artifacts.py (stdlib subset checker) via scripts/feedback_triage.py. `assessment` is whether APODICTIC's own analysis confirmed the external claim; `triage` is the disposition (kept orthogonal to the canonical Must-Fix/Should-Fix/Could-Fix severity scale on purpose). Closed-key (additionalProperties:false) enforced by the engine (Harness Contracts v2): all optional fields (conflicts_with, evidence_refs, maps_to) are declared, so a misspelled field now fails. Declared closed in schemas/_coverage.json. `maps_to` (Increment 2) is the structured finding-id link, cross-checked against the Findings Ledger by feedback-triage. See docs/feedback-triage.md.
title: APODICTIC feedback item (apodictic.feedback_item.v1)

maps_to.$comment: Optional (Feedback Triage Increment 2). A Finding Lifecycle ID (apodictic.finding.v1.id) this feedback item maps to in the Findings Ledger — the structured successor to the prose 'maps to F-…' link. Additive: a feedback item without it stays valid. feedback-triage cross-checks it against the ledger when one is supplied (E5 dangling maps_to = a phantom finding ref; W4 = a `validated` item carrying no maps_to). Same F-<ORIGIN>-<NN> pattern as the finding id it references.
